disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No jaundice (yellow eyes/skin), dark urine/pale stools, confusion/drowsiness, or bleeding/bruising (against hepatic decompensation/encephalopathy)
trigger_question_examples: Any yellowing of your eyes/skin, dark urine or pale stools?
Any confusion, drowsiness, or a flapping tremor of the hands, or easy bruising/bleeding?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No yellowing, my urine and stools are normal, I'm thinking clearly, and no bruising or bleeding.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever/rigors and RUQ tightness is mild and panic-associated (against biliary sepsis)
trigger_question_examples: Any fever or shaking chills, and is the tummy pain severe and constant or a mild tightness?
When did the tightness start relative to the panic?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or chills, the tightness is mild and started with the panic.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began on researching the AMA result; hyperventilating with derealisation and prior health anxiety
trigger_question_examples: When did this start relative to reading about the result?
Does this feel like previous anxiety/panic?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started when I read about it and it feels like my past health anxiety.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Am I going into a liver coma? Do I need hospital tonight?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen jaundice/encephalopathy and biliary-sepsis features; if reassuringly negative, explain that early AMA-positive the slow autoimmune liver condition is very slow and benign (decades) and not failing tonight, de-escalate the panic with breathing, and give a liver safety-net — without dismissing or over-escalating.
